quality.rationale: 通过外微分零条件判定非坐标基下的恰当形式，逻辑严密且可平移至任意标架场/联络框架分析

origin.question: [文件 1: 1_s8_8.2.md]Problem 8.2. The usual basis for polar coordinates, $\mathbf{e}_{\hat{\mathbf{r}}} = \mathbf{e}_{\mathbf{r}}$ , $\mathbf{e}_{\hat{\boldsymbol{\theta}}} = r^{-1}\mathbf{e}_{\boldsymbol{\theta}}$ is not a coordinate basis. Consider the one-form basis $\tilde{\pmb{\omega}}^{\hat{\mathbf{i}}}$ dual to this basis$$< \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathbf {i}}}, \mathbf {e}_{\hat {\mathbf {j}}} > = \delta_{\mathbf {j}}^{\mathbf {i}}.$$Find the function $f$ such that $\tilde{\omega}^{\hat{\mathbf{r}}} = \widetilde{\mathbf{df}}$ and prove that there does not exist a function $g$ such that $\tilde{\omega}^{\hat{\boldsymbol{\theta}}} = \widetilde{\mathbf{dg}}$ . Do this without imposing any metric on the polar coordinates.
origin.answer: $f = r$；不存在满足 $\tilde{\omega}^{\hat{\boldsymbol{\theta}}} = \widetilde{\mathbf{dg}}$ 的函数 $g$
origin.solution: Solution 8.2. It is easy to see that $\mathbf{f}$ is just the coordinate $\mathbf{r}$ , that is $\widetilde{\mathrm{df}} = \widetilde{\mathrm{dr}}$ , since$$\begin{array}{l}< \widetilde {\mathrm {d r}}, \mathbf {e}_{\hat {\mathbf {r}}} > = < \widetilde {\mathrm {d r}}, \mathbf {e}_{\mathbf {r}}> = 1 \\< \widetilde {\mathrm {d r}}, \mathbf {e}_{\widehat {\theta}} > = \mathrm {r}^{- 1} < \widetilde {\mathrm {d r}}, \mathbf {e}_{\theta}> = 0. \\\end{array}$$Now suppose there exists a g such that $\widetilde{\mathrm{dg}} = \tilde{\omega}^{\hat{\theta}}$ then$$\begin{array}{l}0 = \langle \widetilde {\mathrm {d g}}, \mathbf {e}_{\hat {\mathbf {r}}} \rangle = \langle \widetilde {\mathrm {d g}}, \mathbf {e}_{\mathbf {r}} \rangle = \partial \mathbf {g} / \partial \mathbf {r} \\1 = < \widetilde {\mathrm {d g}}, \mathrm {e}_{\hat{\theta}} > = \mathrm {r}^{- 1} < \widetilde {\mathrm {d g}}, \mathrm {e}_{\theta}> = \mathrm {r}^{- 1} \partial \mathrm {g} / \partial \theta . \\\end{array}$$Clearly the conditions $\partial \mathbf{g} / \partial \mathbf{r} = 0$ and $\partial \mathbf{g} / \partial \theta = \mathbf{r}$ are incompatible.
